goal: Preserve the tuned chunked content baseline while adding clean aligned content chunks through conservative curriculum sampling.
reason: The best curriculum candidate nearly matches the tuned baseline and supports the expanded vocabulary, but it still does not beat the baseline on strict text-held-out evaluation.
interpretation: Directly training on many new aligned chunks caused forgetting and reduced strict held-out performance.
Curriculum sampling helps: making original chunks dominate the manifest recovered most of the tuned baseline performance.
The best candidate is the ultra-conservative r10 one-epoch low-learning-rate model, which nearly matches the baseline while keeping the expanded character vocabulary.
Because it still trails the tuned baseline, it should remain experimental rather than becoming the official content checkpoint.
next_step: Use distillation or a dual-head/adapter strategy so the model can learn new aligned chunks without moving the old output behavior as much.
